ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Yok kula ba basi ate ba kowa a nyu daam a buk ate wa lie kpagni a goa ale wa. Wa jam zɛ diipo ate wa liewa a duak ale wa ale diipo ate wa yiti la.
translated: We are all gathered here in the house and we will eat together, then we will go home. We will stay together and eat again later.

ref: When he told his father about this dream, his father became angry and said, “What's that supposed to mean? Are your mother and I and your brothers all going to come and bow down to you?”

text: Sɛm ale Yɛm ale Jafet buuni wie ale nna. Ba jam ka Nowa nidoabilisa. Ba mɛ jam biak ka nidoabilisa nyiamu ale jam tagli tɛŋka la ŋaaŋnyiŋ.

text: Daa ŋata po Faaro ale zak fi zuk a zaani a ŋman pai fu a zaani fi naŋkulaŋa po, ate fi ale pai Faaro beerika a nyo wa nisa po ase fi ale jam a nye diila, ase diipo ate fi jam chim daam teerɔ la.
translated: When the day ends, the farmer goes to the market today with the villagers, but the farmer still works because we need it, so we stay at home, and later we will come back again later here.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Jekob yaa gaari piisaŋa ale buo ŋaai ale paala la a nyini Laban duŋsaŋa po, wa yaa nye ate ŋa a nya buo ŋmiŋmiritaŋa ale pa sobtaŋa ate Laban a soa la, ate ŋa biak buduk ŋmiŋmirita ale pabiik sobta. Wa nye dela kama ate wa duŋsaŋa a piisi ate wa gaari si a nyini Laban duŋsaŋa po.
translated: Jacob bought a new big car and went to Laban’s farm, and he gave the car to Mimirita who lives near Laban’s house, and Buduk Mimirita was also there. He said that he would sell the car when he returned to Laban’s farm.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekob mɛ yaa lɔ wa suik po a cheŋ, ate Wen maarɔma yaa tu wa suik.
translated: Jacob is going to the market today, and when the woman also goes to the market.
